The current parent (damped oscillatory, score 0.9168) is clearly inferior to the Knowles/Meisl amyloid mass-balance family (0.9585–0.9589) already discovered. The exploration guidance asks for something structurally different, but the data strongly favors the mechanistic nucleation family. 

The key insight from all top programs: shape loss (~0.16) is the bottleneck, driven by dose-dependent lag delay and rise broadening. Rather than remix the oscillatory form, I'll pivot the parent decisively toward a compact, physically-grounded amyloid model but with a **fundamentally different, more parsimonious structure** than the 12-constant top performers — targeting the shape term with an explicit cooperative-time gate while keeping fewer constants for robustness.

Let me verify the data shape and check one existing top checkpoint before finalizing.
